description: When you hear these keywords in a problem statement, your brain should instantly light up with 'Builder pattern!'

triggers.keyword: optional fields / some required some optional
triggers.example: Notification with optional image, optional action buttons, optional scheduling
Users can customize their meal with various toppings and sizes
Build the query step by step: select, from, where, orderBy
Order has items, shipping address, payment, coupon, gift wrap, insurance, notes
HttpRequest, Configuration objects that should not change once built
If gluten-free bun, bacon is not available. Card payment requires billing address

notStrategy.keyword: based on type / create different types at runtime
notStrategy.reason: That's selecting WHICH type to create, not configuring HOW to build one type. Factory = WHICH. Builder = HOW.

notStrategy.keyword: swap behavior on an existing object
notStrategy.reason: Strategy swaps algorithms on an existing context. Builder creates a NEW object step by step.

notStrategy.keyword: clone an existing object with modifications
notStrategy.reason: Prototype copies from an existing object. Builder constructs from scratch.

litmusTest: If the QUESTION is 'how do I construct this complex object with many optional parts and validate it before use?' -- think Builder.
